exp.overview: Sun Princess is the first of Princess Cruises' Sphere-Class ships and the line's largest and most contemporary vessel, billed by Princess as 'the most impressive, luxurious and stunning Love Boat ever created.' Its signature architectural flourish is The Dome, a geodesic glass-enclosed structure crowning the top of the ship and inspired by the hillside terraces of Santorini. At the heart of the ship, the central Piazza is reimagined around the outward, suspended Sphere - the namesake of the class - wrapping guests in expansive ocean views and an indoor-outdoor, open-concept flow. Across 21 decks the ship packs 30 restaurant and bar venues, an unprecedented roster of celebrity culinary collaborators, and technically advanced entertainment spaces.
exp.who_for: Squarely aimed at Princess' relaxed-premium sweet spot: couples chasing romance on 'The Love Boat,' and multi-generational families drawn by the dedicated youth centers, Movies Under the Stars and a huge spread of dining. The exclusive Sanctuary Collection tier layers on quiet luxury for guests who want private dining, a top-deck retreat and an all-inclusive Premier bundle, while foodies are courted by a deep bench of specialty restaurants from celebrity partners.

exp.overview: Star Princess is the second Sphere-Class ship and the sister to Sun Princess, sharing the same eye-catching architecture that Princess calls its 'most impressive, luxurious and stunning Love Boats ever created.' It carries the signature elements of the class - the geodesic, glass-enclosed Dome crowning the ship (inspired by the terraces of Santorini) and the central Piazza reinvented around the outward, suspended Sphere. Star adds a round of enhancements over its sister, including expanded dining venues, new leisure experiences and larger non-smoking casino areas. Spread across 21 decks, it offers 30 restaurant and bar venues and the same technically advanced entertainment spaces.
exp.who_for: Like its sister, tuned for Princess' relaxed-premium crowd: romance-seeking couples on 'The Love Boat' and multi-gen families served by dedicated kids', tweens' and teens' clubs, a sports court and Movies Under the Stars. The Sanctuary Collection tier targets guests wanting quiet luxury - suite/mini-suite living with an exclusive Sanctuary Restaurant, private retreat and the all-inclusive Premier bundle. Star leans a touch more toward gaming and dining guests thanks to expanded non-smoking casino areas and enlarged specialty restaurants.

exp.overview: Discovery Princess is the newest Royal-class ship, offering sweeping views from more than 1,400 balconies including 270-degree views from its Sky Suites (the line's largest balconies at sea), the adults-only Sanctuary, and innovative dining such as The Catch by Rudi. It carries 3,660 guests across 19 decks at 145,000 tons and had its inaugural cruise on March 27, 2022. New productions in its state-of-the-art Princess Theater and the MedallionClass experience round out the offering.
exp.who_for: Couples and families seeking the most recent Royal-class ship; frequently deployed to Alaska, making it a strong pick for scenery-focused voyages, as well as couples wanting the top-tier Sky Suites.

exp.overview: Sky Princess is a Royal-class ship that Princess describes as elevating the line's contemporary design 'to even loftier heights,' with its most exciting entertainment venues, newest dining choices, award-winning chef partnerships, and more staterooms than earlier Royal-class ships. It carries 3,660 guests across 19 decks at 141,000 tons, and entered service in October 2019 (christened by Apollo 13 flight controller Poppy Northcutt and Captain Kay Hire). The ship centers on the three-story Piazza atrium and offers the full spread of Princess signature dining and the adults-only Sanctuary retreat.
exp.who_for: Couples, multigenerational families and first-time cruisers who want the newest Royal-class amenities; strong for warm-weather Caribbean and European sailings with a lively but refined onboard atmosphere.

exp.overview: Majestic Princess is a Royal-class ship originally built for the Asian market, carrying 3,560 guests across 19 decks at 143,700 tons, with its inaugural cruise on March 31, 2017 (named by Yao Ming and Ye Li). Its signature difference from its sisters is the Hollywood Pool Club, a glass-domed covered pool and bar with a 'Hollywood chic' environment usable year-round in any weather. It still delivers Princess favorites such as Crown Grill and The Catch by Rudi around the Piazza atrium.
exp.who_for: Couples and families who want a Royal-class ship with a distinctive covered, all-weather pool club; well suited to cooler-weather itineraries where the enclosed Hollywood Pool Club is an asset.

exp.overview: The official page calls Diamond Princess 'a luxury destination in itself' and 'a treasure trove of exceptional delights waiting to be discovered.' Guests can dine on freshly prepared sashimi in Kai Sushi, watch street performers in the dazzling Piazza, or take in a lavish production show in the state-of-the-art theater. exp.overview: Sapphire Princess invites guests to set their sights on the wonders of the world, with more than 700 balconies, world-class gourmet dining and dazzling entertainment.
